page: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine powered by a 48-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this server features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by a 1843 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 85051.53 and Geekbench software compilation with Clang reaching 39975.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Redis SET operations also demonstrate high efficiency at 62.24 million operations per second. However, single-core capabilities are more modest; the Geekbench single-core score of 1117.0 and text processing score of 1414.0 fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory bandwidth remains balanced, with uncached read speeds at 176.14 GB/sec and write speeds at 76.83 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48plds_v5 offers a high density of physical ARM cores relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. The primary tradeoff is the lower memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and weaker single-threaded execution speeds. This hardware profile is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, Redis key-value databases, and static web serving, while being less optimal for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded legacy applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring a 48-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz on the arm64 architecture. It provides 48 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM and a 1843 GB local disk. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation and Redis SET operations, which both rank in the top tier. Conversely, single-core performance is weaker, as evidenced by lower Geekbench single-core scores. With a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, this instance offers a cost-effective, high-density compute profile for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software development pipelines, static web serving, and distributed caching, where physical core density is prioritized over high per-core memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 Ampere Altra cores, 96.0 GB of RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk, offering strong multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v5 is an ARM-based instance with 48 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM-based server featuring forty-eight dedicated physical cores and local storage for highly parallelized, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and a 1843 GB local storage disk
Delivers strong multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations
Features dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and caching
